title: streptanthus cooperi
intro: Streptanthus cooperi, also known as the Cooper's jewelflower, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the mustard family (Brassicaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The health benefits of Streptanthus cooperi include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its high concentrations of glucosinolates and other bioactive compounds. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, although its use is largely anecdotal and more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
